title: Urospermum dalechampii
intro: Urospermum dalechampii, also known as Dale's sticky everlast, is a perennial plant species in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant properties, making it a valuable remedy for various health issues, including wound healing, gastrointestinal problems, and respiratory infections. The bioactive constituents of Urospermum dalechampii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Urospermum dalechampii is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or ointment, and is also used in the form of poultices and infusions to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Urospermum dalechampii is classified as a species within the genus Urospermum, which is a part of the family Compositae. The Compositae family, also known as Asteraceae, is a large and diverse group of flowering plants. Urospermum dalechampii is a biennial plant native to the Mediterranean region. The classification of Urospermum dalechampii can be broken down as follows: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Asterids, order Asterales, family Compositae, genus Urospermum, species U. dalechampii. This classification reflects the plant's evolutionary relationships and its position within the plant kingdom.
